channel.title: Comments for page "You Ain't No Family"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "You Ain't No Family"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
truth be told, i didnt know this existed until a friend of mine who isnt into scp said to me today "idk much about scp but i remember there was a tv head gamer girl scp" and here i am. crying

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I wasn't aware of the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a tale about SCP-3090.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I see why this is on +100 or so. It's a very sweet story, and I really enjoyed reading it. But…. what does that have to do with SCPs or the Foundation? It's an upvote of course, but I'm kinda confused. (+1)

item.title: Re: One of the best tales I've read.
item.content:encoded:
if shes coded in the way i think she is

Very belated response, but yes she is.

What the fuck when did this hit 100?

Thank you all so much. Fourth tale of mine to hit that high, and IMO this is my favorite one I've written. I'm glad you all love Heather. We both wish you a happy new year.

item.title: One of the best tales I've read.
item.content:encoded:
This is what i love about GAW. apart from the humor, is how, unlike most of the wiki's detached and cold rhetoric, they approach things in a personal and emotional way. and the thing is, alot of non-GAW tales do that too. but what makes GAW unique is how modern it is. they even point that out in the writing guide. it deals with modern problems and modern people. and this leads into why i like heather so much as a character. to put it simply, she's relatable.

I can empathize with those feelings of not knowing your own identity and the way you view the people around you. and down to an even more explicit level. she's trans, and you wrote that in in a way that felt natural and added to the story, rather than being a malignant tumor that was stuck in for brownie points (which im going to assume is because you are writing from personal experience). and along side that, if shes coded in the way i think she is (and i mean that as in, narrative coding. not whatever sort of in-universe techno-whatever she's got going on) and implied to have what i think she has (the phrase "sensory overload" was used. but at the same time you never said it explicitly so im not gonna jump to conclusions) than this is also one of the best works ive seen writing that into a character effectively. (case in point, the amount of parenthesized gushing im doing is indicative of how little i see characters like this).

overall , it comes together to create a character that very viscerally feels like a real person, someone that you can project yourself onto and relate to as the event of the story progress.

that ended up being a bit long, but words really cannot describe how much i like this and how much i look forward to what you do with this character in the future

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love her so much she is precious I wanted to cry over what happened to her beforehand oh god she is so precious thank you ;-;

+1 for precious TV head trans girl and another +1 for the mainlist article through sheer association

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's purposefully ambiguous. I don't like stating horrific certainties when it comes to writing trans characters so that's the most I'm willing to say.

I'm glad you like this and Heather :) It means the world to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow damn u got me crying over a cute tv head trans girl I love her so much please enjoy all the +1s I can give (so just the 1, and the 1 on the original article).

There's just one thing I wasn't totally clear about, was the implication that Heather killed herself before her soul was found by GAW? That was where my mind first went, having been there myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As always, these comments mean the world to me, and continues to motivate me to actually take my time and write. I'm so glad you and others enjoy this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been a reader of the SCP wiki for years now and never thought to make an account, but this is the page that finally pushed me to join.

Really, thank you for this, it's a great story and finding a trans-positive corner in one of my favorite places on the internet means so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is truly, heartwarmingly beautiful. I love Gamers Against Weed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad this was one of the first things I read going down the GAW rabbithole. It's definitely one of the most poignant pieces I've read on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Comments like these always reinforce my decision to finish writing this tale and posting it. Thank you (and everyone who this applies to, really) for enjoying it and being able to relate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OP i want you to know i love you and i love this story i am genuinely crying at how sweet it all is and how relatable it is and! trans gay rights!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If there's a Heather Mason Protection Squad, I want to join up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: good
Thank you everyone. Here's to 50 more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Happy to see more GAW content. When I got started reading GAW it felt like the misters were a bit out of character and dubiously moral for them, so I'm happy to see the human motivation expanded upon. Plus it's just a lovely well written tale overall! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That said, if I should make that clearer somehow, I'll gladly take suggestions for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is written well but there is one thing that I'm not sure I'm getting. Was 3090 just walking around freely with its anomalous effects visible?
Edit: I just reread it. I just missed the part that explained it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is very nice and now i udnerstand it

so now she's always had the name heater mason that's nice i'm drowsy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What Passes as Normal was the tale that got me into GAW, it’s pretty rad to have another tale about dahlia and andressa, especially one this good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. The original article felt like it had a story, but it wasn't adressed enough and just added to give weight to a GAW article. This totally changed my perspective on it. This is amazng characterization and I think its a great companion piece. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This does a wonderful job of fleshing out SCP-3090's character, it's heartbreaking and heartwarming and despite the fantastical situation, it's real as shit.

Heartily upvoted, and swapped to an upvote on the SCP as well, since taking this as a companion piece more than compensates for what I felt was missing in the skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
wow. this story was so believable in the actions of the characters and stuff. this was really good.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's weird that nobody has commented on this yet.

I think it's pretty clear how I generally respond to this sort of thing, so color me surprised when I found not just the scenery engaging, but also the characters and their motivations as well. Parts of the story are so fanciful and endearing you can't help but just tear into them, which makes the darker parts that much more expressive. You can feel the dull ache of her heart throughout those sections, you can almost see the glassy look in her eyes. It's very good.

The ending is also abruptly self-aware. I thought I was annoyed by it at first, but the more I thought about it the more it spoke to me. I don't know how meta that shit is supposed to be, but it's almost the theme of the entire piece.

"Hey… Andy."

"Hmm?"

"We have no idea what the fuck we're doing."

"Yeah."

If all of these were this aware of themselves, I don't know. I just think this is as closely connected to a few lines of text as I've felt in a while.

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to the following:

  • Taffeta and The Great Hippo for their critique and suggestions, particularly regarding the final sections of the tale. Made it a lot better.
  • scarhaver for letting me read her Mr. Destiny draft again so I could repick up on minor concepts and themes from that to utilize in this.
  • kinchtheknifeblade because SCP-3012 is thematically super good when writing about this particular aspect.

Couple of things to note:

  • All of this is canon to SCP-3090.
  • "Arcade Hare" is a dual reference to Danganronpa 2 (Arcade Rabbit; spoiler warning there, I suppose) and the Arcade Bunny. I couldn't think of a cleaner way to do it.
  • Most dates are intentionally kept vague to keep flexibility.
  • Andressa is referred to as "mommy", Dahlia as "mom".
  • I'll put more here as I think of them.

Music Listened To While Writing: iwrestledabearonce's It's All Happening, Lingua Ignota's All Bitches Must Die, Sete Star Sept's Beast Mode, and Kid Cudi's Passion Pain & Demon Slayin'

Happy Pride Month!
